juno-hair-garosugil.name: JUNO HAIR Garosugil
juno-hair-garosugil.summary: Garosugil branch of Seoul's largest salon chain — a reliable first cut, with 50+ Klook bookings.
juno-hair-garosugil.bestFor: a reliable first haircut in Seoul
juno-hair-garosugil.whyWeLikeIt: As the Garosugil branch of Seoul's largest salon chain, it offers the kind of predictability that matters on a first visit, and more than fifty bookings have already come through Klook.
juno-hair-garosugil.longDescription:
JUNO HAIR's Garosugil branch sits on Garosugil in Sinsa-dong, Gangnam, and belongs to Seoul's largest salon chain. The offering is straightforward hair styling, and the branch has logged more than fifty bookings through Klook — a useful signal if you are weighing where to book a first appointment.

The chain maintains a website at junohair.com and a main Instagram account, @junohair_official. Booking ahead through Klook is the simplest route in.

color-place-k-pop-celeb-personal-color.name: Color Place
color-place-k-pop-celeb-personal-color.summary: The K-pop celebrity personal-color studio — famous for idol visits, from BLACKPINK to TWICE.
color-place-k-pop-celeb-personal-color.bestFor: K-pop fans seeking an idol-visited studio
color-place-k-pop-celeb-personal-color.whyWeLikeIt: The client list is the credential — idols from BLACKPINK to TWICE have visited, and 202 reviews hold the rating at a flat 5.0.
color-place-k-pop-celeb-personal-color.longDescription: Color Place is the Gangnam studio best known for its K-pop clientele — idols from BLACKPINK to TWICE have visited for personal-color analysis, and it holds a 5.0 rating across 202 reviews. The service is a single specialty, color diagnosis, offered under a brand with a considerable public profile: an official site at colorplace.co.kr and an Instagram following of more than fifty thousand at @colorplace_official. Check the website for session details and booking before you go.

color-signal-personal-color-makeup.name: Color Signal
color-signal-personal-color-makeup.summary: Thorough color analysis with interpreter service; reviewers note the team follows through even past closing time.
color-signal-personal-color-makeup.bestFor: visitors who want an interpreter on hand
color-signal-personal-color-makeup.whyWeLikeIt: Reviewers praise both the thoroughness of the analysis and the interpreter service, and one account describes the team seeing things through diligently even after closing time. A 4.9 average across more than 900 reviews backs the pattern up.
color-signal-personal-color-makeup.longDescription: Near Gangnam Station, Color Signal has built one of the larger review bases in this category — a 4.9 average across more than 900 ratings. Accounts describe a thorough analysis and single out the interpreter service, which removes the language barrier from what is essentially a long conversation about your own coloring. One review mentions a booking-time mix-up that the team resolved by working diligently past closing. Session information is on the official site, colorsignal.co.kr. If the interpreter matters to your visit, confirm that arrangement when you reserve.

colorize-personal-color-makeup-gangnam.name: Colorize (Gangnam)
colorize-personal-color-makeup-gangnam.summary: Gangnam branch of the Colorize personal-color studio — same brand as the Myeongdong flagship.
colorize-personal-color-makeup-gangnam.bestFor: the Colorize brand south of the river
colorize-personal-color-makeup-gangnam.longDescription: This is the southern branch of Colorize, sharing its brand with the Myeongdong flagship but sitting near Gangnam Station instead — convenient if your itinerary keeps you below the river. The branch holds a 4.9 average across 320 reviews of its own. Details for both locations run through the shared website, colorize.co.kr, and the Instagram account @colorize_you. For most travelers the choice between the two studios comes down to geography; the flagship appears separately in this guide.

earthness-spa-aroma-massage.name: Earthness Spa
earthness-spa-aroma-massage.summary: Quiet aroma-oil massage studio in Gangnam.
earthness-spa-aroma-massage.bestFor: a calm aroma-oil massage in Gangnam
earthness-spa-aroma-massage.longDescription: A quiet aroma-oil massage studio in the Gangnam district, Earthness Spa is listed for a single specialty — aromatherapy massage — and holds a 4.9 rating from a small set of 19 reviews. Little else about the studio is published online, so it is worth confirming hours and availability before you go.

in-spa-premium-hotel-spa.name: IN SPA
in-spa-premium-hotel-spa.summary: Premium hotel-style spa and massage in Gangnam.
in-spa-premium-hotel-spa.bestFor: a hotel-style spa session in Gangnam
in-spa-premium-hotel-spa.longDescription: In the Gangnam district, IN SPA offers spa and massage treatments in the premium hotel style its Korean name promises. It carries a 4.8 rating across 73 reviews. Details of the treatment menu are not widely published in English, so booking ahead — and confirming what each course includes — is a sensible first step.

moclock-scalp-hair-loss-care.name: Moclock Scalp Care
moclock-scalp-hair-loss-care.summary: Scalp and hair-loss care specialist head spa in Gangnam.
moclock-scalp-hair-loss-care.longDescription: Moclock is a head spa in Gangnam with a specific focus: scalp and hair-loss care. It holds a 4.4 rating across 32 reviews. Our records list only a district-level address and no website or Instagram, so confirm the exact location, hours, and prices through a map listing before making the trip.

myshopper-personal-color-shopping-guide.name: Myshopper
myshopper-personal-color-shopping-guide.summary: Personal color analysis paired with a guided shopping session.
myshopper-personal-color-shopping-guide.bestFor: turning a diagnosis into a shopping trip
myshopper-personal-color-shopping-guide.longDescription: Myshopper pairs a personal-color analysis with a guided shopping session in Gangnam, so the diagnosis carries directly into a shopping trip built around the results. The public record is modest — a 4.7 rating from 11 reviews, with no website or Instagram account listed — so treat the details as worth confirming through your booking channel before you commit to a time.

cutloose-k-pop-hair-makeup.name: Cutloose
cutloose-k-pop-hair-makeup.summary: Cheongdam K-pop hair and makeup studio; the director's creative styling made their Instagram the portfolio.
cutloose-k-pop-hair-makeup.whyWeLikeIt: A perfect 5.0 across 34 reviews, and the director's creative styling has made the studio's Instagram its de facto portfolio — worth scrolling before you decide.
cutloose-k-pop-hair-makeup.longDescription:
Cutloose is a K-pop hair and makeup studio on the first floor at Cheongdam-dong 93-14. There is no website; the studio's Instagram accounts — @cutloose_kr and the director's own @cutloose_woo_, followed by about 62,000 people — function as both portfolio and point of contact. Reviews stand at a full 5.0 across 34 entries.

Since no external booking channel is listed, reaching out through Instagram is the practical way to arrange an appointment.

normalshop.name: Normalshop
normalshop.summary: Hair and makeup studio near Gangnam-gu Office Station — pets welcome.
normalshop.bestFor: an appointment your pet can join
normalshop.whyWeLikeIt: Pets are welcome here, a considerate detail if you are traveling with a companion animal.
normalshop.longDescription: A hair and makeup studio on the third and fourth floors at Seolleung-ro 753, near Gangnam-gu Office Station in the Cheongdam area. The distinguishing note in our record is that pets may accompany their owners. No ratings or reviews are on file yet, so set expectations accordingly; the studio's Instagram, @normalshop_official, is the place to confirm details before visiting.

rolling-jay.name: Rolling Jay
rolling-jay.summary: Hair and makeup salon between Cheongdam and Gangnam-gu Office Station.
rolling-jay.bestFor: hair and makeup in the Cheongdam area
rolling-jay.longDescription: Between Cheongdam-dong and Gangnam-gu Office Station, Rolling Jay handles both hair and makeup from its studio at Cheongdam-dong 39-14. There is no website; the salon posts at @rollingjay_official, while its makeup director runs @rollingjay_onymakeup with a following of about 75,000. No ratings or reviews appear in our record, so treat the Instagram feeds as your best preview and point of contact.

juno-hair-gangseo-office.name: JUNO HAIR Gangseo
juno-hair-gangseo-office.summary: Gangseo branch of the JUNO HAIR chain.
juno-hair-gangseo-office.longDescription: In the Gangseo district of Seoul, this JUNO HAIR branch offers the chain's standard hair services. Our record lists no street address, rating, or reviews for this location, so check junohair.com or the main Instagram account, @junohair_official, to confirm the exact address and hours before making the trip.

hosu-dosan.name: HOSU DOSAN
hosu-dosan.summary: Premium K-pop hair and makeup on Dosan-daero; idol-style makeovers, bookable via Creatrip.
hosu-dosan.whyWeLikeIt: Idol-style makeovers are the house specialty, holding a 4.6 rating across 19 reviews, and appointments can be arranged through Creatrip.
hosu-dosan.longDescription:
On the fourth floor at Dosan-daero 50-gil 13 in Cheongdam, HOSU DOSAN positions itself as a premium K-pop hair and makeup studio, with idol-style makeovers as the signature offering. The studio maintains two Instagram accounts, @hosudosan_official and @hosudosan_makeup_official.

Reviews average 4.6 across 19 entries. For visitors, the practical route in is Creatrip, which handles bookings for the studio.

a3-nature-private-body-scrub.name: A3 Nature
a3-nature-private-body-scrub.summary: Private Korean body-scrub spa in Dongdaemun.
a3-nature-private-body-scrub.longDescription: A3 Nature is a private body-scrub spa in Dongdaemun, offering the Korean scrub in a private setting rather than a communal bathhouse. Public information about the shop is limited, so confirm hours and whether a reservation is required before visiting.

sparex-sauna-good-morning-city-dongmyo.name: SPAREX Sauna
sparex-sauna-good-morning-city-dongmyo.summary: Classic jjimjilbang sauna in the Good Morning City building near Dongmyo.
sparex-sauna-good-morning-city-dongmyo.longDescription: Inside the Good Morning City building near Dongmyo, in the Dongdaemun area, SPAREX is a classic Korean sauna and jjimjilbang — the communal bathhouse format rather than a private spa, and a fit for travelers curious about everyday Korean sauna culture. Published details are sparse, so check current hours before planning a visit around it.

a-muu-hair-salon.name: A:MUU Hair Salon
a-muu-hair-salon.summary: A 28-year veteran director — layered cuts, perms, and head spa, popular with visiting travelers.
a-muu-hair-salon.bestFor: layered cuts and perms from a veteran director
a-muu-hair-salon.whyWeLikeIt: The director brings 28 years of experience to layered cuts, perms, and head spa work, and the salon holds a 4.9 rating across 60 reviews.
a-muu-hair-salon.longDescription:
Three floors up at Myeongdong 8na-gil 47, A:MUU Hair Salon is led by a director with 28 years of experience, and the menu centers on layered cuts, perms, and head spa treatments. Foreign travelers make up a noticeable share of the clientele, according to reviews, and the salon holds a 4.9 rating across 60 of them.

The salon posts at @amuuhair_myeongdong, and the director keeps a personal account at @jyj8371; either works as a preview before you book.

anskin-myeongdong.name: ANSKIN
anskin-myeongdong.summary: Facial care studio in the heart of Myeongdong.
anskin-myeongdong.bestFor: a straightforward facial in central Myeongdong
anskin-myeongdong.longDescription: ANSKIN is a facial-care studio in the heart of Myeongdong. The listing is brief — no website or Instagram is on record — but its rating stands at a full 5.0 across 29 reviews. With so few online details available, confirm opening hours and availability locally before planning your day around a visit.

art-de-la-peau.name: Art de la Peau
art-de-la-peau.summary: Esthetic facials and massage — one of Myeongdong's best-reviewed skincare rooms.
art-de-la-peau.whyWeLikeIt: A 4.9 average across 487 reviews is a rare depth of feedback for a neighborhood skincare room, and it backs the studio's standing as one of Myeongdong's best-reviewed.
art-de-la-peau.longDescription: Few Myeongdong skincare rooms carry a track record like this one. Art de la Peau pairs esthetic facials with massage, and its 4.9 average across 487 reviews makes it one of the district's best-reviewed places for skin care. The studio sits in central Myeongdong, within Seoul's Jung-gu. No website or Instagram is listed in our record, so plan to confirm booking details on the ground.

cocorea-makeup-hair.name: Cocorea
cocorea-makeup-hair.summary: Personal color plus hair and makeup in one sitting; bookings run through Klook and Naver.
cocorea-makeup-hair.bestFor: diagnosis and a makeover in one sitting
cocorea-makeup-hair.whyWeLikeIt: Folding hair and makeup into the same appointment as the diagnosis is a practical format for travelers on a schedule.
cocorea-makeup-hair.longDescription: Cocorea's premise is efficiency: a personal-color diagnosis followed by hair and makeup in the same sitting, at a first-floor studio on Myeongdong 4-gil. It holds a 4.6 rating from 146 reviews. There is no official website — reservations run through Klook and Naver instead — and the studio's Instagram account is @cocoreamakeup. The format has a clear logic for visitors: rather than taking your results home as theory, you walk out already styled to the palette you were just given.

colorize-personal-color-analysis-makeup.name: Colorize (Myeongdong)
colorize-personal-color-analysis-makeup.summary: The Colorize flagship — consultants explain why each color works, down to shopping picks. 2,400+ reviews.
colorize-personal-color-analysis-makeup.bestFor: understanding the reasoning behind your palette
colorize-personal-color-analysis-makeup.whyWeLikeIt: The numbers are striking — 2,400-plus reviews at 4.9 — but the substance is the explanation: clients leave understanding why their palette works, with shopping picks to act on it.
colorize-personal-color-analysis-makeup.longDescription: With more than 2,400 reviews holding a 4.9 average, the Colorize flagship on Toegye-ro is among the most-reviewed studios in this category. What clients single out is the teaching: consultants such as Dana and Lucy explain why a particular color works on you rather than simply naming your season, and the session carries through to concrete shopping recommendations. Makeup is offered alongside the diagnosis. Details are on the official site, colorize.co.kr, and the same brand operates a second branch near Gangnam Station if the south side of the city suits your plans better.

hana-mud-spa.name: Hana Mud Spa
hana-mud-spa.summary: Women-only mud sauna and massage with Korean-style amethyst sauna rooms; book via Creatrip or Klook.
hana-mud-spa.whyWeLikeIt: A women-only format with mud and amethyst sauna rooms is a distinctive combination, and booking through Creatrip or Klook keeps reservations simple for visitors.
hana-mud-spa.longDescription: Hana Mud Spa is a women-only spa on the fifth floor of a building on Myeongdong 8-gil, combining a mud sauna, Korean-style and amethyst sauna rooms, and massage. It holds a 4.5 rating across 62 reviews. Reservations run through Creatrip or Klook, which makes booking straightforward for English-speaking visitors; note the women-only policy before planning a visit in mixed company.

herique-hair.name: Herique Hair
herique-hair.summary: Well-reviewed Myeongdong hair salon with 460+ ratings.
herique-hair.whyWeLikeIt: A 4.6 average across more than 460 ratings is a substantial sample for a single salon, which lends the score real weight.
herique-hair.longDescription: Herique Hair occupies a fifth-floor space at Myeongdong 10-gil 18. It is one of the most-reviewed hair salons in our Myeongdong records, with a 4.6 average across more than 460 ratings. Beyond haircuts, the record lists no further service detail, so consult the salon's Instagram, @herique.official, for current work and to make contact — no website or booking platform is listed.

juno-hair-myeongdong-street.name: JUNO HAIR Myeongdong
juno-hair-myeongdong-street.summary: Myeongdong Street branch of the JUNO HAIR chain — hair and head spa.
juno-hair-myeongdong-street.bestFor: pairing a haircut with a head spa
juno-hair-myeongdong-street.whyWeLikeIt: A 4.6 rating across 154 reviews suggests the big-chain consistency holds at this branch, and the head spa menu adds a reason to stay beyond the cut.
juno-hair-myeongdong-street.longDescription:
The Myeongdong Street location is one of several JUNO HAIR branches in the neighborhood — the chain operates many outposts across Seoul, each with its own Instagram account alongside the head-office @junohair_official. This branch offers hair services and head spa treatments and carries a 4.6 rating across 154 reviews.

The branch account is @juno_myeongdong2, and junohair.com covers the chain as a whole. With multiple Myeongdong locations, confirm which branch you have booked before you set out.

line-esthe-facial-care.name: Line Esthe
line-esthe-facial-care.summary: Facial-care room in Myeongdong with consistently high ratings.
line-esthe-facial-care.whyWeLikeIt: Consistency is the draw here: a 4.9 average across 158 reviews suggests the experience holds up visit after visit.
line-esthe-facial-care.longDescription: In central Myeongdong, Line Esthe keeps its focus squarely on facial care, and its ratings have stayed consistently high — a 4.9 average across 158 reviews at last count. That consistency is the main reason it appears here. Our record lists no website or Instagram, so confirm hours and availability locally rather than relying on online booking.

mizo-massage-rooftop-spa.name: MIZO Rooftop Spa
mizo-massage-rooftop-spa.summary: Rooftop spa combining full-body, foot, and facial care.
mizo-massage-rooftop-spa.bestFor: covering body, foot, and face in one visit
mizo-massage-rooftop-spa.longDescription: MIZO combines full-body, foot, and facial treatments at a rooftop spa in Myeongdong, so a single booking can cover all three. It rates 4.7 across 95 reviews, and the location is easy to fold into a Myeongdong afternoon. Details beyond that are limited, so confirm course options and timing when booking.

myeongdong-beauty-skyview-spa.name: Myeongdong Beauty Skyview Spa
myeongdong-beauty-skyview-spa.summary: Hot stone, full-body massage, and facial with a rooftop view, one minute from Myeongdong Station.
myeongdong-beauty-skyview-spa.bestFor: a massage with a rooftop view
myeongdong-beauty-skyview-spa.whyWeLikeIt: A 4.9 rating across 435 reviews is hard to argue with, and reviewers consistently mention well-judged pressure and friendly staff rather than only the view.
myeongdong-beauty-skyview-spa.longDescription: One minute on foot from Myeongdong Station, this spa pairs a hot-stone and full-body massage with a facial, taken in with a rooftop view. It holds a 4.9 rating across 435 reviews, and Korean reviews repeatedly note moderate, well-judged pressure and friendly staff. Find it on Instagram at @mdbty77; the station-adjacent location makes it easy to slot in at the end of a day in the neighborhood.

myeongdong-condition-body-head-massage.name: Myeongdong Condition
myeongdong-condition-body-head-massage.summary: Body and head massage studio in Myeongdong.
myeongdong-condition-body-head-massage.longDescription: Myeongdong Condition is a massage studio in Myeongdong focused on full-body and head massage. It carries a 4.8 rating from 227 reviews, and the head-massage option is the reason to pick it when head and scalp tension are the main complaint. Beyond the rating, published details are thin, so confirm hours and courses directly before visiting.

myeongdong-skinspa.name: Myeongdong Skinspa
myeongdong-skinspa.summary: Korean-style facials, RF lifting, and skin boosters at the Skinspa flagship.
myeongdong-skinspa.bestFor: facials plus RF lifting and skin boosters
myeongdong-skinspa.whyWeLikeIt: The menu reaches past the standard facial — Korean-style facial care sits alongside RF (radio-frequency) lifting and a range of skin boosters at the brand's flagship.
myeongdong-skinspa.longDescription: The flagship of the Skinspa brand sits in Myeongdong and covers more than a basic facial menu: Korean-style facial care, RF (radio-frequency) lifting, and a range of skin boosters. Our record carries no rating data for this branch, so the most current picture comes from its Instagram, @skinspa_korea, which has around 4,400 followers. If you are considering a device-based treatment such as RF lifting, it is worth messaging ahead to check what is available.

o-hui-whoo-spa-massage.name: O HUI & WHOO Spa
o-hui-whoo-spa-massage.summary: Spa and massage built around the O HUI and WHOO cosmetic lines — 1,000+ reviews.
o-hui-whoo-spa-massage.bestFor: fans of O HUI and WHOO products
o-hui-whoo-spa-massage.whyWeLikeIt: More than a thousand reviews at a 4.6 average is unusual volume, and treatments built on named cosmetic lines make it easy to know what is being used on your skin.
o-hui-whoo-spa-massage.longDescription: This Myeongdong spa builds its treatments around the O HUI and WHOO Korean cosmetic lines and has accumulated more than 1,000 reviews — 1,007 at a 4.6 average at last count. For visitors already loyal to either brand, being treated with the products themselves is the draw. Given that volume of reviews, booking ahead is advisable.

parkjun-beautylab.name: Parkjun BeautyLab
parkjun-beautylab.summary: Total beauty lab known for its signature 15-step scalp treatment; a favorite among visiting travelers.
parkjun-beautylab.whyWeLikeIt: Foreign visitors know the brand for its signature 15-step scalp treatment, and the operation runs largely on Instagram; on Klook, the Seongsu branch holds a 5.0, albeit from just nine reviews.
parkjun-beautylab.longDescription:
Parkjun BeautyLab is a total-beauty operation spanning hair, head spa, and makeup, with locations including a Myeongdong studio on the fifth floor of the Solaria Hotel at Myeongdong 8-gil 27 and a Gangnam branch about five minutes on foot from Sinnonhyeon Station exit 5. Among visiting travelers the brand is best known for its signature 15-step scalp treatment.

The Myeongdong and Gangnam branches run their own Instagram accounts — @parkjunbeautylab and @parkjunbeautylab_gangnam — alongside the head-office account. The Seongsu branch is listed on Klook, where it holds a 5.0 from nine reviews.

spa-1978-red-ginseng-ritual.name: SPA 1978
spa-1978-red-ginseng-ritual.summary: Red-ginseng ritual spa (formerly SPA 1899) in Myeongdong.
spa-1978-red-ginseng-ritual.whyWeLikeIt: The red-ginseng ritual gives the visit a clearly Korean identity, and a 4.7 average over 341 reviews suggests the concept is more than branding.
spa-1978-red-ginseng-ritual.longDescription: SPA 1978, which previously operated as SPA 1899, anchors its Myeongdong treatments in a red-ginseng ritual. It rates 4.7 across 341 reviews. If you want a treatment that feels specifically Korean rather than generically relaxing, the ginseng focus is the reason to choose it; course details are best confirmed when booking.

the-bom-personal-color-makeup.name: The Bom
the-bom-personal-color-makeup.summary: Personal color and makeup studio on Toegye-ro, near Myeongdong.
the-bom-personal-color-makeup.longDescription: The Bom is a personal color and makeup studio on Toegye-ro 18-gil, within easy reach of central Myeongdong, and holds a 4.8 rating across 179 reviews. Its website, thebom.day, and Instagram account, @studio_thebom_, are the places to check session formats and current availability. Beyond the strong rating, little detail is on public record, so confirm specifics directly if your schedule depends on them.

ye-lead-hair-styling.name: Ye_Lead Hair
ye-lead-hair-styling.summary: Premium salon built around an 18-step signature scalp treatment.
ye-lead-hair-styling.whyWeLikeIt: The salon has built its identity around an 18-step signature scalp treatment, and a 4.8 rating across 123 reviews suggests the ritual holds up.
ye-lead-hair-styling.longDescription: On the third floor at Myeongdong 8ga-gil 26, Ye_Lead positions itself as a premium hair salon whose centerpiece is an 18-step signature scalp treatment. The salon holds a 4.8 rating from 123 reviews, and its Instagram account, @ye_lead, is the most direct window into current work. There is no official website listed, so checking Instagram before you go is the practical first step.

argyol-seongsu.name: Argyol
argyol-seongsu.summary: K-beauty styling bar with a 15-step head spa; four branches across Seoul.
argyol-seongsu.bestFor: pairing a styling session with a head spa
argyol-seongsu.whyWeLikeIt: A 15-step head spa is an unusually thorough menu item for a styling bar, and the brand runs four branches across Seoul.
argyol-seongsu.longDescription: Argyol runs four branches across Seoul — Myeongdong, Gangnam, Hongdae, and this one in Seongsu — under a K-beauty styling concept anchored by a 15-step head spa. Several branches keep their own Instagram accounts (@a.rgyol_seongsu for the Seongsu location), so the branch page is the most direct place to check current work. No ratings or review counts are published for this location, so confirm details through the branch account before booking.

jungsaemmool-k-beauty-makeup-class-seong.name: JUNGSAEMMOOL Makeup Class
jungsaemmool-k-beauty-makeup-class-seong.summary: JUNGSAEMMOOL's group makeup class with 1:1 touches — take home a fan-size product and use the photo booth after.
jungsaemmool-k-beauty-makeup-class-seong.bestFor: a group K-beauty makeup class with 1:1 touches
jungsaemmool-k-beauty-makeup-class-seong.whyWeLikeIt: The format blends a group class with one-on-one touches, so you get something close to a private tutorial without the private price. A fan-size product to take home and a photo booth at the end are thoughtful extras.
jungsaemmool-k-beauty-makeup-class-seong.longDescription:
In Seongsu, the JUNGSAEMMOOL brand runs its K-beauty makeup class out of the Seongsu 101 space in Seongdong-gu. The session pairs a group format with one-on-one touches, which reviewers say delivers much of the effect of a private tutorial at a group-class price. At the end, each participant takes home a fan-size product and can step into the on-site photo booth.

The class holds a 4.7 rating across 141 reviews. There is no website listed, so the Instagram accounts @jsmbeauty_101_seongsu and @jungsaemmool11 are the place to look for current details.

mércdi-hair-salon.name: Mércdi Hair Salon
mércdi-hair-salon.summary: Small Seongsu hair salon; official web presence and exact location unverified.
mércdi-hair-salon.bestFor: a haircut in Seongsu, once confirmed
mércdi-hair-salon.longDescription: Mércdi is a small hair salon in Seongsu-dong that currently holds a 5.0 rating from a dozen reviews. Its public footprint is thin: the Instagram account (@mercdi_) is limited, no official website has been confirmed, and the exact address is also unverified — searches tend to surface an unrelated fashion brand with a similar name instead. Until those details are checked, confirm the location directly before making the trip.

whipped-face-mask-cleanser-class.name: WHIPPED
whipped-face-mask-cleanser-class.summary: One-day vegan class in Seongsu — whip up your own cleanser and face mask.
whipped-face-mask-cleanser-class.bestFor: making your own vegan cleanser and mask
whipped-face-mask-cleanser-class.whyWeLikeIt: Whipping your own vegan cleanser and face mask is a different kind of beauty outing in Seongsu, and a 4.9 average across 37 reviews suggests the class delivers.
whipped-face-mask-cleanser-class.longDescription: Over in Seongsu, WHIPPED runs a one-day vegan beauty class where you make your own cleanser and face mask rather than buying one. It is an experience rather than a treatment — you leave with something you whipped up yourself. The studio sits at 16 Seongsui-ro 20-gil in Seongdong-gu, and its rating stands at 4.9 across 37 reviews. Class schedules and sign-up details are best checked through its Instagram, @whipped._official, which counts roughly 75,000 followers.

the-foret-spa-seoul-forest.name: THE FORET SPA

korean-home-style-cooking-class-near-gan.whyWeLikeIt: Rather than marching through a fixed recipe, the class leaves room to improvise, and reviewers value learning the cultural background of each ingredient. A 4.9 rating across 80 reviews backs it up.
korean-home-style-cooking-class-near-gan.longDescription:
This home-style class near Gangnam Station takes a loose approach to recipes: rather than following one line by line, participants are given room to improvise while preparing a main dish alongside several banchan. Along the way, the cultural background of each ingredient is explained, so the session doubles as a small lesson in Korean food culture.

It holds a 4.9 rating across 80 reviews, and the location by Gangnam Station makes it straightforward to reach by subway.

kimchi-making-class-with-chef-hakyung-at.name: Kimchi Class with Chef Hakyung
kimchi-making-class-with-chef-hakyung-at.summary: Kimchi-making class with Chef Hakyung near Gyeongbokgung.
kimchi-making-class-with-chef-hakyung-at.longDescription: Led by Chef Hakyung, this kimchi-making class takes place near Gyeongbokgung Palace in Jongno-gu, in the Bukchon area. The session is devoted entirely to kimchi, and the location is simple to pair with a morning at the palace or a walk through Bukchon afterward.

cooking-class-in-mangwon-market-with-mic.name: Mangwon Market Chef's Cooking Class
cooking-class-in-mangwon-market-with-mic.summary: Shop Mangwon Market with a Michelin-experienced chef, then cook what you found — closer to cultural exchange than a lesson.
cooking-class-in-mangwon-market-with-mic.bestFor: market-to-table cooking with a professional chef
cooking-class-in-mangwon-market-with-mic.whyWeLikeIt: Reviewers describe it as closer to cultural exchange than a lesson — shopping Mangwon Market with Chef Pano before cooking together — and the clean, spacious studio draws its own praise.
cooking-class-in-mangwon-market-with-mic.longDescription:
At Mangwon Market in Mapo-gu, Chef Pano — a chef with Michelin restaurant experience — begins the class with a walk through the market stalls, shopping for the day's ingredients before heading back to cook them together. Reviewers describe the format as closer to a cultural exchange than a formal lesson, and note that the studio is clean and spacious.

The class holds a 5.0 rating across 28 reviews; updates are posted on Instagram at @seoulfoodventure and @seoulcookingclub.

korean-cooking-class-with-grocery-shoppi.name: Market-to-Table Cooking Class
korean-cooking-class-with-grocery-shoppi.summary: Korean cooking class that starts with grocery shopping in Mangwon Market.
korean-cooking-class-with-grocery-shoppi.bestFor: shopping and cooking like a local
korean-cooking-class-with-grocery-shoppi.longDescription: The session begins not at a stove but in the aisles of Mangwon Market in Mapo-gu, where participants shop for groceries before cooking a Korean meal with what they found. It is a straightforward market-to-table format; plan for the shopping portion as part of the class time rather than a separate activity.

kimchi-cooking-class-in-traditional-hano.name: Hanok Kimchi Cooking Class
kimchi-cooking-class-in-traditional-hano.summary: Kimchi class held in a traditional hanok near Bukchon.
kimchi-cooking-class-in-traditional-hano.bestFor: making kimchi inside a traditional hanok
kimchi-cooking-class-in-traditional-hano.longDescription: Held in a traditional hanok house in Jongno-gu, near Bukchon and Gyeongbokgung, this class is devoted to a single subject: kimchi. The hanok venue is the distinguishing detail, and the location keeps the palace and the rest of Bukchon close at hand.

royal-seoul-cooking-class-in-a-100-year-.name: Royal Seoul Cooking Class
royal-seoul-cooking-class-in-a-100-year-.summary: Royal Korean cooking in a 100-year-old hanok, paired with a market tour.
royal-seoul-cooking-class-in-a-100-year-.bestFor: royal recipes and a market tour
royal-seoul-cooking-class-in-a-100-year-.whyWeLikeIt: Ninety-eight reviewers rate it 5.0, with particular praise for the instructors' background knowledge and considerate pacing.
royal-seoul-cooking-class-in-a-100-year-.longDescription:
A century-old hanok in the Bukchon area is the setting for this class, which pairs royal-style Korean cooking with a tour of a local market. Reviewers praise the instructors — Park is mentioned by name — for their depth of background knowledge and considerate pacing.

It holds a 5.0 rating across 98 reviews, and the hanok sits near Gyeongbokgung in Jongno-gu, so it combines easily with a palace visit. Updates are posted at @royalseoulcc on Instagram.

traditional-korean-6-dish-cooking-class-.name: Korean 6-Dish Class & Alley Tour
traditional-korean-6-dish-cooking-class-.summary: Six-dish traditional Korean cooking class plus a hidden-alley walking tour.
traditional-korean-6-dish-cooking-class-.bestFor: a full Korean table plus neighborhood walk
traditional-korean-6-dish-cooking-class-.longDescription: Six traditional Korean dishes are on the menu here, cooked in a single session before the group heads out for a walking tour of alleys near Bukchon and Gyeongbokgung in Jongno-gu. Because the class and the tour come as one combined program, plan your schedule around both parts rather than the cooking alone.

seoul-soju-makgeolli-tasting-experience.name: Soju & Makgeolli Tasting
seoul-soju-makgeolli-tasting-experience.summary: Soju and makgeolli tastings, from distillery visits to guided flights.
seoul-soju-makgeolli-tasting-experience.bestFor: a guided introduction to soju and makgeolli
seoul-soju-makgeolli-tasting-experience.longDescription:
These tastings introduce Korea's traditional alcohols — soju and makgeolli — in formats that range from guided flights to distillery visits in Seoul. Session titles such as "Seoul Soju Tasting at Distillery" and "Discover Traditional Soju & Makgeolli" give a sense of the spread on offer.

Because the formats differ, it is worth confirming whether your session is a seated tasting or a distillery visit before you book.

where-locals-eat-korean-bbq-dry-aged-han.name: Where Locals Eat: Korean BBQ
where-locals-eat-korean-bbq-dry-aged-han.summary: Hosted Korean BBQ and dry-aged hanwoo dinners where locals actually eat.
where-locals-eat-korean-bbq-dry-aged-han.bestFor: diners curious about dry-aged hanwoo beef
where-locals-eat-korean-bbq-dry-aged-han.longDescription:
Hosted dinners built around Korean BBQ and dry-aged hanwoo beef, held at the kind of restaurants where locals actually eat. Formats vary — listings such as "Korean BBQ of the Month," "Private Korean BBQ with Jason," and "A Gourmet Korean BBQ & Dry-Aged Hanwoo" suggest both private and gourmet-dinner versions of the experience.

One practical note: the venue's location is not publicly disclosed, so plan around receiving the specifics through the booking rather than mapping it out in advance.

brewing-makgeolli-experience.name: Makgeolli Brewing Experience
brewing-makgeolli-experience.summary: Brew your own makgeolli in a Seongsu workshop.
brewing-makgeolli-experience.bestFor: brewing your own Korean rice wine
brewing-makgeolli-experience.longDescription: Over in Seongsu-dong, Seongdong-gu, this workshop centers on brewing makgeolli, Korea's traditional rice wine, and each participant makes their own batch. As a traditional-liquor workshop rather than a cooking class in the usual sense, it suits visitors who have already tried a kimchi or banchan class and want to go one step further.

the-seoul-pub-crawl.name: The Seoul Pub Crawl
the-seoul-pub-crawl.summary: Itaewon-based pub crawl through Seoul's nightlife.
the-seoul-pub-crawl.bestFor: a sociable evening out in Itaewon
the-seoul-pub-crawl.longDescription: Based in Itaewon, in Yongsan-gu, this pub crawl moves a group through Seoul's nightlife over the course of an evening and currently holds a 4.7 rating. Published details beyond the Itaewon base are limited, so check the current schedule and meeting point when booking — and expect a late-night, social format rather than a quiet tasting.

gwangjang-market-half-day-street-food-ex.name: Gwangjang Street Food Tour
gwangjang-market-half-day-street-food-ex.summary: Half-day Gwangjang Market street-food tour run like a stamp-rally challenge — guides get repeat praise.
gwangjang-market-half-day-street-food-ex.bestFor: a first guided taste of Gwangjang Market
gwangjang-market-half-day-street-food-ex.whyWeLikeIt: Reviewers keep returning to the guides — Cecilia's local knowledge and friendliness come up again and again — and the tour holds a 4.9 rating across 179 reviews.
gwangjang-market-half-day-street-food-ex.longDescription:
A half-day tour through Gwangjang Market in Jongno, structured as a stamp-rally challenge that works through the market's signature street foods, with a souvenir folded into the format. This is Klook's own tour, booked directly through that platform.

With a 4.9 rating across 179 reviews, and guides repeatedly praised for their local knowledge and warmth, it offers a structured way to take on the market rather than guessing on your own. Set aside a half day for the full route.

gwangjang-market-half-day-vegan-food-tou.name: Gwangjang Vegan Food Tour
gwangjang-market-half-day-vegan-food-tou.summary: Half-day vegan food tour through Gwangjang Market.
gwangjang-market-half-day-vegan-food-tou.longDescription:
Plant-based visitors get a route of their own through Gwangjang Market with this half-day tour, which currently holds a 5.0 rating. The premise is straightforward: a guided walk through the Jongno market focused entirely on vegan food.

Published details are otherwise limited, so confirm the current lineup of stops — and any dietary specifics that matter to you — directly when booking.

absolute-seoul-pub-crawl-hongdae-itaewon.name: Absolute Seoul Pub Crawl
absolute-seoul-pub-crawl-hongdae-itaewon.summary: High-energy pub crawl with hosts who mix an international crowd — Hongdae most nights, Itaewon on Fridays.
absolute-seoul-pub-crawl-hongdae-itaewon.bestFor: meeting fellow travelers on a night out
absolute-seoul-pub-crawl-hongdae-itaewon.whyWeLikeIt: With a 4.8 rating across 263 reviews, the crawl is praised for local hosts who run the night with real energy and pull travelers of many nationalities into one group.
absolute-seoul-pub-crawl-hongdae-itaewon.longDescription:
Absolute Seoul Pub Crawl runs on a fixed weekly rhythm: Hongdae on Thursdays, Saturdays, and Sundays, and Itaewon on Fridays. Local hosts keep the energy high and mix travelers of many nationalities into one group — reviewers describe it as much a way to meet fellow travelers as a bar tour.

The crawl has a website at seoulpubcrawl.com and an Instagram account at @absolutekorea; check which district is on before you head out, since the night's location depends on the day of the week.

olive-young-n-seongsu.name: Olive Young N Seongsu
olive-young-n-seongsu.summary: Olive Young's five-floor innovation flagship, opened in late 2024 near Seongsu Station — the chain's biggest bet yet on what a K-beauty store can be.
olive-young-n-seongsu.bestFor: a one-stop overview of what's trending across Korean beauty right now
olive-young-n-seongsu.whyWeLikeIt: It's less a bigger branch than a lab for the brand's next store format, with each floor built around a different way of shopping rather than just more shelves.
olive-young-n-seongsu.longDescription:
Olive Young N Seongsu opened in November 2024 as the chain's first entry in a new 'innovation store' concept, spread across five floors near Exit 4 of Seongsu Station. Where a standard Olive Young branch is organized by product category, this flagship is organized by experience — different floors are built around different ways of browsing and testing K-beauty products.

As the format-setter for where Olive Young's retail concept is headed next, it's worth the detour even for travelers who've already been to a dozen branches elsewhere in Seoul.

amore-seongsu.name: Amore Seongsu
amore-seongsu.summary: Amorepacific's flagship experience space in a converted repair shop — test 2,000+ products across 30+ brands, no checkout pressure.
amore-seongsu.bestFor: Trying the whole Amorepacific universe before you buy
amore-seongsu.whyWeLikeIt: It's built as a showroom, not a store — plus free luggage lockers, which every carry-on traveler eventually thanks them for.
amore-seongsu.longDescription:
Amore Seongsu occupies a former auto repair shop converted into Amorepacific's group-wide showroom, letting visitors test products from more than 30 of the company's brands in one open space rather than shopping brand by brand. With well over 2,000 products on hand, it's built for exploration rather than a fast checkout.

Free luggage lockers make it a practical stop mid-itinerary, and the space sits alongside several other beauty and fashion flagships on Seongsu's Yeonmujang-area streets.

tamburins-seongsu.name: Tamburins Seongsu
tamburins-seongsu.summary: A raw-concrete flagship built around Tamburins' perfumes, hand creams, and rotating art installations, on one of Seongsu's original converted-warehouse streets.
tamburins-seongsu.bestFor: browsing niche Korean fragrance without a hard sell
tamburins-seongsu.whyWeLikeIt: The architecture is the draw as much as the products — the cave-like concrete interior has made it a fixture in Seongsu's design-tourism circuit since it opened.
tamburins-seongsu.longDescription:
Tamburins Seongsu is the fragrance and cosmetics brand's flagship on Yeonmujang 5-gil, built inside a raw-concrete structure that leans as hard into architecture as it does into product. Alongside the brand's perfumes, hand creams, and body care, the space regularly hosts art installations that change out the interior look.

It's open daily from 11am to 9pm, a few minutes from Seongsu Station Exit 4, and works well paired with the other flagship stores clustered on the same block.

nonfiction-seongsu.name: Nonfiction Seongsu
nonfiction-seongsu.summary: A minimalist showroom for Nonfiction's fragrance and skincare line, with open counters built for unhurried testing rather than a quick grab-and-go.
nonfiction-seongsu.bestFor: sampling Nonfiction's signature scents at your own pace
nonfiction-seongsu.whyWeLikeIt: The pared-back, gallery-like layout matches the brand's whole aesthetic — it reads more like a design showroom than a retail counter.
nonfiction-seongsu.longDescription:
Nonfiction's Seongsu store is one of more than ten global locations for the fragrance and skincare brand, but it's the flagship showroom for Seoul. The interior favors open testing counters and a restrained material palette over dense product walls, in keeping with the brand's minimalist identity.

It keeps daily hours from 11am to 8:30pm and sits a short walk from Tamburins and other Seongsu flagships, making it an easy stop on a fragrance-focused route through the neighborhood.

tirtir-seongsu.name: TIRTIR Seongsu
tirtir-seongsu.summary: TIRTIR's two-floor flagship, built around its cult-favorite cushion foundation shade range — five distinct zones for matching your exact tone before you buy.
tirtir-seongsu.bestFor: finding your shade match among TIRTIR's wide cushion foundation range
tirtir-seongsu.whyWeLikeIt: The 'Shade Valley' concept turns a task that's usually a guessing game online — picking the right cushion shade — into something you can actually test in person.
tirtir-seongsu.longDescription:
TIRTIR Seongsu opened in mid-2025 as the brand's flagship, spread across a basement and ground floor near Seongsu Station on Yeonmujang 5-gil. The store is organized into five themed zones under a 'Shade Valley' concept, built specifically around trying the brand's wide range of cushion foundation shades in person.

The store passed its first anniversary with a dedicated event, a reasonable signal it's a standing flagship rather than a short-lived promotional pop-up. Hours run 10:30am to 8:30pm daily.

amuse-seongsu.name: Amuse Seongsu
amuse-seongsu.summary: Amuse's all-pink 'Pink House' flagship — a full brand-world showroom for its vegan, jelly-textured makeup line, opened in mid-2025.
amuse-seongsu.bestFor: the most photographed makeup showroom in Seongsu right now
amuse-seongsu.whyWeLikeIt: It commits fully to one color story instead of splitting the difference, which makes it read as a genuine brand statement rather than a generic pop-up shell.
amuse-seongsu.longDescription:
Amuse opened its Seongsu flagship — nicknamed 'Pink House' — in June 2025, its second flagship store after an earlier Hongdae location. The roughly 200-square-meter space is built entirely around the brand's signature pink and its vegan, jelly-textured makeup and skincare line.

Expect a dense photo-op interior alongside testing stations for Amuse's core products; it's a quick stop rather than a long browse, but a distinctive one on a Seongsu beauty crawl.

fwee-seongsu.name: fwee Seongsu
fwee-seongsu.summary: fwee's first offline store, nicknamed 'AGIT' — built around testing its full lip-tint and pudding-pot color range in one blue-toned space.
fwee-seongsu.bestFor: swatching fwee's full lip color lineup in one visit
fwee-seongsu.whyWeLikeIt: Dozens of shades laid out for open testing is a genuinely useful format for a brand best known online for color range — you can actually compare them side by side here.
fwee-seongsu.longDescription:
fwee AGIT Seongsu, opened in February 2024, is the makeup brand's first permanent offline store after a run of pop-ups, including one at The Hyundai Seoul. The store leans into fwee's signature blue on its exterior and gives shoppers open access to test dozens of lip product shades and the brand's full pudding pot lineup.

It keeps year-round daily hours from 11am to 9pm and sits a short walk from several of Seongsu's other beauty flagships, making it easy to fold into a same-day beauty-store route.

musinsa-standard-seongsu.name: Musinsa Standard Seongsu
musinsa-standard-seongsu.summary: The physical storefront for Musinsa's in-house basics label, letting you check sizing and fabric on pieces most travelers only know from the app.
musinsa-standard-seongsu.bestFor: checking Musinsa Standard sizing and fit before ordering more online
musinsa-standard-seongsu.whyWeLikeIt: It turns Korea's biggest fashion platform from a screen into a fitting room — genuinely useful if you plan to keep shopping Musinsa's app after you fly home.
musinsa-standard-seongsu.longDescription:
Musinsa Standard Seongsu is the two-floor retail store for Musinsa's own basics line, part of a broader wave of offline stores the online platform has opened in the neighborhood alongside its larger Musinsa Megastore Seongsu. The space stocks minimalist, everyday casualwear in neutral colorways, the label's signature look.

It's open daily from 11am to 9pm with tax-free service for international visitors, making it a practical stop if you already shop the Musinsa app and want to check fit in person.

ader-error-seongsu.name: Ader Error Seongsu Space
ader-error-seongsu.summary: Ader Error's flagship since 2020, renovated for the brand's 10th anniversary into a chrome-and-glass, gallery-like two-floor space.
ader-error-seongsu.bestFor: seeing Ader Error's off-kilter design language applied to a whole building, not just a rack of clothes
ader-error-seongsu.whyWeLikeIt: The renovation treats the store as an art installation first and a shop second, which fits a brand that's always blurred the line between fashion and conceptual art.
ader-error-seongsu.longDescription:
ADER Space has anchored Ader Error's presence in Seongsu since 2020, and was fully renovated in late 2024 for the brand's tenth anniversary under the theme 'FUTOURIST.' The redesign leans into glass and chrome finishes with undulating interiors and futuristic fixtures across two floors, alongside the brand's ready-to-wear and accessories.

Open daily from 1pm to 9pm, it's one of the longer-standing flagships in the neighborhood rather than a newcomer, and rewards a slower browse over a quick in-and-out.

lcdc-seoul.name: LCDC Seoul
lcdc-seoul.summary: A four-floor complex stacking a café, a multi-brand select shop, and several independent fashion labels under one roof — Seongsu's version of a vertical mall.
lcdc-seoul.bestFor: browsing several independent Korean fashion labels without hopping between separate stores
lcdc-seoul.whyWeLikeIt: Each floor has its own distinct identity instead of blending into one generic retail floor, so it rewards working your way up rather than just hitting the ground level.
lcdc-seoul.longDescription:
LCDC Seoul stacks a ground-floor café (Cafe Ephemera) with a curated multi-brand select shop on the second floor and several independent labels — including Doors, Geulwoll, and Apoteka Soul — sharing the third, plus a jewelry-and-accessories floor above. The building itself, a converted structure on Yeonmujang 17-gil, is part of the draw alongside what's inside it.

Hours vary a little by floor, roughly 10am to 9pm depending on the tenant, with valet parking available — useful given how spread out Seongsu's flagship stores are.

eql-grove-seongsu.name: EQL Grove
eql-grove-seongsu.summary: EQL's offline entry point into Seongsu, an 'editorial forest' select shop that brings the online platform's curated fashion picks into a physical space with rotating local brand collaborations.
eql-grove-seongsu.bestFor: discovering smaller Korean labels alongside EQL's own curated picks
eql-grove-seongsu.whyWeLikeIt: It functions as a community space as much as a shop — the rotating collaborations with local Seongsu brands give it a reason to check back on repeat visits.
eql-grove-seongsu.longDescription:
EQL Grove brought the online curated-fashion platform EQL into a physical Seongsu storefront, styled as an 'editorial forest' that showcases items previously only available through the app. Beyond straightforward shopping, the space runs themed promotions and collaborations with other local Seongsu brands.

It's open daily from 11am to 8pm, closed on Korean public holidays, and works well as a stop for travelers who want a curated, editor's-pick alternative to the bigger flagship stores nearby.

matin-kim-seongsu.name: Matin Kim Seongsu
matin-kim-seongsu.summary: Matin Kim's Seongsu flagship for the label's oversized, Y2K-leaning streetwear — one of several Seoul stores, but the one built as a full brand-world showroom.
matin-kim-seongsu.bestFor: trying on Matin Kim's oversized silhouettes in person before buying online
matin-kim-seongsu.whyWeLikeIt: It's one of Matin Kim's larger physical footprints, worth the trip if you already follow the brand's fast-moving online drops and want to check fit for yourself.
matin-kim-seongsu.longDescription:
Matin Kim runs several storefronts across Seongsu, and this flagship on Yeonmujang 3-gil is the label's main showroom for the neighborhood, alongside smaller branch and special-edition stores nearby. The brand is known for oversized, Y2K-inflected streetwear that moves quickly through frequent online drops.

Seeing the fit and fabric in person is the main draw here, since Matin Kim's popularity online means pieces can sell out fast — a fitting-room visit is a low-risk way to confirm sizing before committing to a purchase.

onion-seongsu.name: Onion Seongsu
onion-seongsu.summary: The original Onion — a converted factory turned bakery café that's widely credited with kicking off Seongsu's transformation into Seoul's café district.
onion-seongsu.bestFor: warehouse-industrial atmosphere plus a genuinely strong bakery counter
onion-seongsu.whyWeLikeIt: It predates most of the cafés that copied its look, and it still bakes well enough to be worth the visit even without the exposed-brick backdrop.
onion-seongsu.longDescription:
Onion Seongsu occupies a former factory building and is often credited as the café that put Seongsu-dong on the map as a destination neighborhood, well before the area filled with flagship fashion stores. The bakery counter, not just the industrial-chic interior, is the reason regulars keep returning — bread and pastries sell out on busy days.

It opens earlier than most Seongsu cafés, from 8am on weekdays and 10am on weekends, both running until 10pm — useful if you want to beat the crowds that build later in the day.

mesh-coffee.name: Mesh Coffee
mesh-coffee.summary: A compact, takeout-only hand-drip specialty bar near Seoul Forest — no seating, just a tight focus on the coffee itself.
mesh-coffee.bestFor: a quick, high-quality coffee stop on the walk to or from Seoul Forest
mesh-coffee.whyWeLikeIt: Dropping seating entirely lets it focus purely on the brew — a useful counterpoint if you've spent the day in Seongsu's photo-op cafés and just want good coffee, fast.
mesh-coffee.longDescription:
Mesh Coffee is a small, takeout-only specialty bar between Seoul Forest and Ttukseom Station, built around hand-drip coffee rather than a sit-down café experience. There's no indoor seating, so it works best as a stop on the way to or from Seoul Forest park rather than a destination to linger at.

Weekday hours run 10am to 6pm; the matcha latte and Americano are the most-mentioned menu items, and its small footprint means it can get busy quickly around peak café-hopping hours.

in-the-pink-seongsu.name: In The Pink Seongsu
in-the-pink-seongsu.summary: An English-first personal color studio in Seongsu, run by a bilingual founder who has draped 9,000-plus clients through a 350-plus-swatch process.
in-the-pink-seongsu.bestFor: travelers who want the whole session — booking form to drape to breakdown — handled comfortably in English
in-the-pink-seongsu.whyWeLikeIt: Founder Nicole built the entire booking flow, and the FAQ that explains bare-face draping, around clients who don't speak Korean — down to the wording of the questions people actually ask before a session.
in-the-pink-seongsu.longDescription:
In The Pink is a personal color studio in Seongsu run by Nicole, a certified color consultant and fashion stylist who has led personal color workshops abroad through the Korean Cultural Center, including stops in Hungary, the UK, the Czech Republic, Italy, and Spain. Sessions center on a bare-face draping process using more than 350 fabric swatches to identify a client's undertone, then extend into recommendations for makeup shades, hairstyle and color, glasses frames, and fabric choices.

Booking runs through a form-and-email flow rather than a walk-in counter, with 1:1 sessions lasting about 80 minutes and paired sessions running longer — all explained in English from the FAQ onward, which is unusual for the format in Seongsu. The studio's exact address isn't published online; it's shared directly with clients once a booking is confirmed.

ccl-color-lab-seongsu.name: CCL Color Image Convergence Lab
ccl-color-lab-seongsu.summary: A personal-color and image-consulting lab near Seoul Forest, run out of its own Seongsu studio with a full program menu from color draping to body-frame styling.
ccl-color-lab-seongsu.bestFor: a deeper consulting menu — color, body-frame styling, and makeup — under one roof rather than a single-service session
ccl-color-lab-seongsu.whyWeLikeIt: CCL also runs its own academy arm and holds patent registrations for its diagnostic tools, so the person draping you has typically also trained other colour consultants in the method.
ccl-color-lab-seongsu.longDescription:
CCL (컬러이미지융합연구소) runs its personal color studio out of the Seoul Forest Dream Tower in Seongsu, a short walk from Seoul Forest itself. Alongside its core personal color consulting program, the lab offers a men's-focused diagnosis track, body-frame and fit styling, makeup consulting, and color therapy sessions.

What sets it apart from single-session studios nearby is the academy side of the business — CCL holds its own patent registrations for diagnostic tools and issues its own counselor certifications through a dedicated training arm. Sessions can be booked through its Instagram (@cclcolor_official) or arranged directly through the studio.

5x5-nail-studio-seongsu.name: 5x5 Nail Studio Seongsu
5x5-nail-studio-seongsu.summary: A high-following nail art studio in Seongsu known for nature-inspired custom designs — marble, stone, and watercolor motifs — booked entirely through Instagram.
5x5-nail-studio-seongsu.bestFor: elaborate custom nail art booked in advance, with English-speaking staff on hand
5x5-nail-studio-seongsu.whyWeLikeIt: The studio keeps separate accounts for its class offerings and design archive, a level of documentation that's closer to a working design studio than a typical walk-in nail shop.
5x5-nail-studio-seongsu.longDescription:
5x5 Nail Studio (오오네일) runs two Seoul locations, one in Seongsu and one in Yeonnam, and has built a large following around nature-inspired nail art — marble, stone-texture, and watercolor motifs among its signature styles. Appointments, which typically run about an hour to ninety minutes depending on the design, are arranged through Instagram DM or KakaoTalk rather than a booking site.

English-speaking staff support the international clients who make up a large share of its bookings, and the studio maintains sister accounts for its class offerings and design archive — worth a browse before messaging to book, since custom pieces are usually chosen from a reference photo.

dakyo-nail-seongsu.name: Dakyo Nail Seongsu
dakyo-nail-seongsu.summary: The Seongsu branch of the Dakyo Nail family, a K-pop-adjacent nail art studio with its own account and following distinct from the brand's Hannam and Apgujeong locations.
dakyo-nail-seongsu.bestFor: trend-driven, idol-inspired nail art in the style Dakyo Nail is known for
dakyo-nail-seongsu.whyWeLikeIt: Being the Seongsu outpost of a multi-branch brand means near-daily fresh design uploads on its own account — a useful scroll for style references before you message to book.
dakyo-nail-seongsu.longDescription:
Dakyo Nail Seongsu (다교네일 성수) is one of several branches under the Dakyo Nail name, a nail art brand also operating in Hannam-dong and — separately from this Seongsu shop — Apgujeong. The Seongsu location runs its own Instagram account distinct from the brand's main and representative accounts, posting frequent updates on current nail art designs.

As with most Seongsu nail studios of this type, booking runs through Instagram DM or a linked Kakao channel rather than a website, and the shop sits in a commercial building on Yeonmujang 15-gil in the heart of the neighborhood's studio cluster.

soonsiki-seongsu.name: Soonsiki Seongsu
soonsiki-seongsu.summary: The Seongsu branch of Soonsiki, a multi-location Seoul hair chain built around English-friendly service for international guests.
soonsiki-seongsu.bestFor: a full-service cut, color, or perm handled by a chain that markets its English-friendliness up front
soonsiki-seongsu.whyWeLikeIt: Soonsiki brands its English-friendly service and multi-branch consistency directly on its own site rather than leaving it to a DM negotiation, which is unusual for a Seongsu salon at this scale.
soonsiki-seongsu.longDescription:
Soonsiki Seongsu (순시키헤어 성수 샤미즈점) is one of four Seoul locations run under the Soonsiki name, occupying the 4th and 5th floors of a building on Yeonmujang 3-gil, alongside sibling branches in Hongdae. The chain has built its identity around serving international guests specifically, branding itself as English-friendly across its locations.

Booking runs through the salon's own site or its Instagram (@soonsiki.official), and the Seongsu branch keeps the same Wednesday–Sunday, 10am–7pm schedule as the rest of the chain, closed Mondays and Tuesdays.
